mechanism_narrative: An extremely low unemployment rate, particularly when sustained, signals a late-stage economic expansion where labor costs are likely to rise and profit margins compress, increasing the probability of a future slowdown and thus leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are consistently positive (e.g., > 60% positive instances) when UNRATE is below 4.0% over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate rises above a certain threshold, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy aimed at curbing inflation or cooling an overheating economy. This tightening typically increases borrowing costs, dampens economic activity, and reduces equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 55% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 2.5% over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with strong housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, signaling a healthy and expanding economy. This strong demand environment typically translates into higher corporate revenues and earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of periods where both RSAFS and HOUST show positive 3-month growth above 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic recovery, an acceleration in industrial production signifies increasing capacity utilization and business investment, which are strong tailwinds for corporate earnings and equity markets, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is particularly potent when coming out of a trough.
failure_condition: If, during identified post-recession recovery periods, the S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are negative when INDPRO growth exceeds 3% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive real GDP growth indicates a healthy and expanding economy, which generally translates into higher corporate profits and investor confidence, supporting positive S&P 500 forward returns over short horizons.
failure_condition: If the S&P 500 forward 5-day returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration or contraction in non-farm payroll employment indicates a weakening labor market and broader economic slowdown, which typically leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when PAYEMS 3-month average change falls below 50,000.

mechanism_narrative: A combination of rising inflation (CPIAUCSL) and an increasing federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates a period of aggressive monetary tightening to combat overheating. This scenario typically leads to higher discount rates and concerns about economic growth, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 forward 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of periods where both CPIAUCSL inflation exceeds 3.0% and FEDFUNDS has increased by more than 0.5 percentage points over 6 months.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of strong consumer confidence, robust retail sales growth indicates healthy consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns. This effect is amplified when consumer sentiment is high.
failure_condition: If, during periods of high consumer confidence (e.g., University of Michigan Consumer Sentiment Index > 90), S&P 500 forward 5-day returns are negative when RSAFS shows >0.5% 1-month growth.
